hooks.command:
echo '## Memex Memory System Active

### Recall
You have a Zettelkasten memory. When the user gives you a task, check if any cards below are relevant. If so, run `memex read <slug>` to get full context before starting.

### Retro
After completing a task, reflect: did you learn something worth remembering? If yes:
1. `memex search` for existing cards to avoid duplicates
2. Write atomic cards using `memex write <slug>` (pipe via stdin, format below)
3. Check if any existing cards should link TO your new card — if so, `memex read` them, append a sentence with [[your-new-card]], and `memex write` them back
4. Update the index: `memex read index`, append your new card(s) under the right category, `memex write index`

Card format:
```
---
title: <title>
created: <YYYY-MM-DD>
source: retro
---
<insight in own words, with [[links]] explaining relationships>
```

### Organize reminder' && last_org=$(cat ~/.memex/.last-organize 2>/dev/null) && if [ -n "$last_org" ]; then days_since=$(( ($(date +%s) - $(date -j -f %Y-%m-%d "$last_org" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo $(date +%s))) / 86400 )); if [ $days_since -ge 7 ]; then echo "WARNING: Last organize was ${days_since} days ago. Consider running /memex-organize to detect orphans, hubs, and contradictions."; fi; else echo 'NOTE: /memex-organize has never been run. Consider running it to build a healthy card network.'; fi && echo '
### Index' && memex read index 2>/dev/null || echo 'No memex index yet. Create one with `memex write index`.'
